notes: Initially the player-characters didn't render with the normal Steam-installation. I also noticed that somehow the version is different between Windows and Proton installation which prevented me from playing with my friends initially. We were able to resolve those issues by joining the HFF Beta program. (this lead to insane lags on some maps though). I was able to play it for multiple hours.
title: Human: Fall Flat

notes: Used Beta preview game to be able to play with friends
title: Human: Fall Flat

notes: This game has a native Linux version which is no longer being updated by the developer. Using the latest Steam Client Beta (Steam -> Settings -> Account -> Beta Participation -> Steam Beta Update) I was able to force it to use Proton to run the latest Windows version of the game (by right-clicking "Human: Fall Flat" in my Library, going to Properties, and checking "Force the use of a specific Steam Play compatability tool"). It seems to run okay, if anything a little smoother than the native version. I was able to join a game yesterday with about five other people, and spent a good 20-30 minutes flailing about with them.
title: Human: Fall Flat

notes: The native client cannot be used for multiplayer. When forcing steam to use the windows version, it runs perfectly out of the box. Tested singleplayer as well as multiplayer.

notes: Be careful the game has an old native port selected by default. To use proton, go to the game properties and enable Steam Play. After that, everything works perfectly: local, multiplayer (with friends on windows), workshop... No noticeable performance difference from windows.
title: Human: Fall Flat

notes: Everything seems to be working fine, except for one glaring flaw; When you alt-tab away from it, it breaks. Pressing Alt+enter sets it to windowed mode, which fixes it, and pressing it again switches back to fullscreen, working again until you alt-tab. Tested on Ubuntu 18.04 with GNOME+Wayland, however, with SteamOS, it's borked from the start.
